channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Target gets booted from Twin Cities Pride festival after rolling back DEI efforts
item.description: Twin Cities Pride executive director Andi Otto says the nonprofit made the decision because Target is not standing up “against those who are fighting against us.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Ki and The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders tariffs on Colombia over rejection of US military deportation flights
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump said Sunday that he was ordering tariffs, visa restrictions and other retaliatory measures to be taken against Colombia after its government rejected two U.S. military flights carrying migrants.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: List: All of Trump’s executive orders on the economy
item.description: President Donald Trump started his second administration with a flurry of policy actions since returning to the White House on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Palestinian refugee idea falls flat with Jordan and confounds a Senate ally
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s push to have Egypt and Jordan take in large numbers of Palestinian refugees from besieged Gaza fell flat with those countries' governments and left a key congressional ally in Washington perplexed on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alaskans say Trump can change the name of Denali but can't make people call it Mount McKinley
item.description: President Donald Trump has changed the name of Alaska's Denali back to Mount McKinley. It's a nod to President William McKinley, who was from Ohio and who never set foot in Alaska. Many Alaskans say they'll never stop calling the mountain Denali.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As Hegseth takes charge at the Pentagon, here's what changes could be in store
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth starts his first official day on Monday morning, he will face a daunting array of issues to tackle — from global conflicts and border security to administrative tasks.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Colombian leader quickly caves after Trump threats, offers presidential plane for deportation flights
item.description: Gustavo Petro was quickly brought to heel by President Donald Trump after the Colombian leader rejected several deportation flights from the U.S. He is now offering his presidential plane to help with the flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump should rethink revoking former officials' security details, Tom Cotton says
item.description: A top Republican Senate ally says that President Donald Trump should rethink his decision to remove security details from three former senior national security officials, saying their protection is not just for them but also the public.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Graham says Republicans may 'own another attack' on US if Trump border czar doesn't get deportation funds fast
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., said congressional Republicans who delay funding for border czar Tom Homan could "own another attack on our country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump orders tariffs on Colombia over rejection of US military deportation flights
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump says that he's ordering tariffs, visa restrictions and other retaliatory measures against Colombia after its government rejected two military flights carrying migrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dems take ‘government for granted’ on immigration: Chicago alderman
item.description: "Many Democratic leaders refuse to show any kind of working relationship with the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Trump wants Jordan and Egypt to accept more Palestinian refugees and floats plan to 'clean out' Gaza
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump said he would like to see Jordan, Egypt and other Arab nations increase the number of Palestinian refugees they are accepting from the Gaza Strip, potentially moving out enough of the population to “just clean out” the war-torn area to create a virt
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump leans into his pledge to eliminate taxes on tips at a Las Vegas rally
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — President Donald Trump came to Las Vegas on Saturday to crow about his campaign promise to eliminate taxes on tips, signaling to allies in Congress the importance of the policy but stopping well short of offering details for making the slogan a reality.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson invites Trump to address Congress amid busy first 100-day sprint
item.description: In a letter first obtained by Fox News Digital, House Speaker Mike Johnson invited President Donald Trump to address Congress on March 4 to share his 'America First vision for our legislative future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The CIA believes COVID most likely originated from a lab but has low confidence in its own finding
item.description: The CIA now believes the virus responsible for the COVID-19 pandemic most likely originated from a laboratory.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McConnell opposes Hegseth on final confirmation vote
item.description: His vote came as a surprise after he voted Thursday night to advance Hegseth’s nomination to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's 5 biggest moves on immigration so far
item.description: President Trump released a flurry of immigration actions this week, signing a series of orders targeting the border and ramping up enforcement. While Trump has long pledged to address illegal immigration, many of his actions targeted longstanding legal pathways.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump ends federal security detail for Dr. Anthony Fauci
item.description: Fauci is the latest in a string of former Trump aides-turned-critics to see their federal protection canceled.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada state senator takes on gender, sports issue at UNLV, UNR, community colleges
item.description: Republican state Senator Carrie Buck is sponsoring legislation that would require Nevada colleges and universities to restrict participation in athletics based on the athlete's gender at birth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: The new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation, and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration. That's according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Stick with us: Why the internet is obsessed with one of the simplest tools known to man
item.description: "Stick Nation" is a worldwide movement that thrives on irreverent, fantastical and downright wholesome descriptions of sticks.
item.dc:creator: Alina Hartounian, MPR News

item.title: Bishop Mariann Budde, former Minnesota bishop, tells NPR ‘I won’t apologize’ for sermon addressing Trump
item.description: The Episcopal bishop of Washington spoke with NPR to discuss her sermon addressing President Trump and why she asked him to have mercy.
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise, MPR News

item.title: Henderson mayor threatens legal action against councilwoman following ‘spurious rumors’ of extramarital affair
item.description: A litany of personal attacks has enveloped the city of Henderson, and elected officials are coming forward to explain why distrust has taken over a part of city hall.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New York plan would give money to new moms: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York would give hundreds of dollars in financial support to low-income parents under a new proposal. Last week, Gov. Kathy Hochul announced a proposal to give low-income parents a “birth allowance” during pregnancy and at the birth of their child.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5
